page: Vultr voc-s-2c-16gb-480s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, running on the x86_64 architecture. The processing power is delivered by an AMD EPYC processor clocked at 2.0 GHz, configured with 1 physical core and 2 threads per core under a shared CPU allocation model. The system includes 480 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and lacks a dedicated GPU.

Performance benchmarks reveal a mixed capability profile. The instance achieves top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng testing, complemented by strong single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression speeds. However, multi-core CPU performance and uncached memory bandwidth read/write speeds remain in the average tier. Tradeoffs are evident in the weak Passmark CPU and database operation scores, alongside poor static web serving throughput and weak Redis SET performance, which are likely influenced by the shared CPU allocation.

From a resource density perspective, this server offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, paired with a substantial local NVMe SSD. This makes it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require sustained, dedicated CPU performance. Recommended workloads include lightweight caching layers, memory-bound data processing, development environments, and moderate database operations where high storage capacity and memory footprint are prioritized over raw multi-core compute throughput.
description: Vultr voc-s-2c-16gb-480s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance configured with 2 shared vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 480 GB of NVMe SSD storage. Powered by an AMD EPYC processor running at 2.0 GHz, this x86_64 server delivers top-tier single-core performance in stress-ng benchmarks and strong single-threaded compression speeds. However, its shared CPU allocation leads to lower multi-core efficiency, with weak Passmark CPU scores, weak Redis SET performance, and poor static web serving throughput. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy tasks that do not demand continuous dedicated CPU resources. It is best suited for memory-bound data processing, lightweight caching, and development environments.

tagline: A memory-rich virtual server with local NVMe storage, balancing strong single-core execution with shared-resource cost efficiency.

480 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
